title: Boehringer hands over funding, marketing rights for Click’s digital therapeutic
description: Boehringer Ingelheim and Click Therapeutics are restructuring their marketing deal for CT-155, as the German pharma also opens its coffers.

title: Philips recalls Trilogy Evo ventilator software, warns of ‘under-delivery’ of therapy
description: Philips issued a voluntary recall of software for its series of Trilogy Evo ventilators, as well as a warning not to use non-pneumatic nebulizers.

title: J&J launches latest version of its pulsed ablation device after nabbing CE Mark
description: J&J Medtech launched the latest version of its pulsed ablation device after receiving CE Mark approval in Europe.

title: Medline hit with FDA warning letter over defective heart syringes, safety hazards
description: After Medline pulled off a massive $6.26 billion IPO late last year, the company now finds itself facing serious concerns being leveled by the FDA.

title: Artivion nabs PMA clearance for aortic stent graft system, triggers Endospan buyout option
description: Artivion, an Atlanta-based medtech that focuses on cardiac and vascular surgery, received pre-market approval (PMA) for its aortic stent graft system.

title: E2 lands $80M series C for next-gen thrombectomy treatment
description: E2 has raised $80 million in a series C funding round as it looks to commercialize its pulmonary embolism treatment.

title: Zeto’s latest EEG device garners FDA 510(k) clearance for outpatient use
description: Zeto received FDA clearance for an EEG device designed for use in outpatient clinics or at home.

title: Epia Neuro launches with BCI device to help stroke patients rework brain signals
description: Epia Neuro is looking to tap the potential of BCI technology, launching a new device that aims to turn brain signals into digital commands.

title: Whoop raises $575M series G, Abbott comes on board amid hiring spree
description: Whoop is whooping it up with a massive $575 million raise amid a strong start to the year for the fitness wearable.

title: Johnson & Johnson’s Impella pumps miss the mark in 2 major coronary studies
description: Two separate randomized trials failed to illustrate Impella's benefits in specific cases of patients with blocked coronary arteries.
